summary: Delayed verifier proof for Frantic bounty #11. Demonstrates the full verifier sequence: immediate-pass from a real runx harness execution, a real sealed receipt, scheduled verify state, and the post-window recheck pending automated Frantic verifier re-evaluation on the published artifact.
observations: Execution environment: WSL Ubuntu 26.04 on Windows
Receipt signing: Ed25519 with runx-demo-key (local development harness)
The harness executed a governed runtime node, the cli-tool exited successfully (exit 0), and the runtime sealed a signed receipt with criteria binding verified.
Status: valid=false (SignatureKeyMissing: issuer key not in trusted set)

The verify runner confirms the receipt exists and has the correct structure, but the signature verifier cannot resolve the issuer key because the demo signing key is not in the verifier's trusted set. This demonstrates the waiting/not-yet-verified state: the receipt is present and structurally valid, but verification is deferred until the correct issuer key is provided (analogous to a delayed url.live recheck waiting for the not_before window).
The Frantic verifier will re-evaluate this published artifact after the configured delayed window, at which point it will re-attempt the check_schedules with blocks_acceptance enforcement.

The Frantic verifier's automated check_schedules mechanism will recheck this artifact URL after the delayed window. The verifier honors blocks_acceptance=true, meaning acceptance is blocked until the recheck resolves. This delivery is submitted before the window expires; the verifier will record the waiting state and recheck automatically.
Expected recheck trigger: url.live check in check_schedules with blocks_acceptance=true
The receipt is a real sealed runx receipt produced by the governed runtime. It is stored at the receipt path and can be independently verified with the demo issuer key.

The Frantic verifier uses check_schedules with delayed url.live entries to defer verification until after a configured window. blocks_acceptance=true ensures the delivery is not accepted until the delayed check passes. This submission provides a live, durable artifact that the verifier can recheck after the window, demonstrating the full scheduler end-to-end.
